svg.title: The one-page superintendent's memo — three moves, zero new budget
svg.desc: A mockup of a one-page administrative memo from a superintendent stacking three prescribed moves. Move one: an administrative letter recognizing HarvardX, MITx, Stanford Online, and Carnegie Mellon completion at a published credit ratio (signals learning is valued). Move two: a study-group documentation pathway using existing PLC structure with a one-page monthly template (signals trying is valued). Move three: an AI-fluency salary-increment tier with peer review borrowed from National Board Certification (signals practice change is valued). Each move is annotated with "new budget required: $0" and the signal it sends.
svg.text: OFFICE OF THE SUPERINTENDENT
MEMORANDUM
TO: All certificated staff, principals, PD coordinators
FROM: Superintendent
RE: Recognition of validated AI professional development
DATE: Effective immediately · No new budget required
Three administrative decisions, one signature
Issue an administrative letter recognizing completion.
Validated AI training — HarvardX DPI 681M, MITx, Stanford Online, Carnegie Mellon
equivalents — fulfills professional-development hours under existing district policy.
Published per-course credit ratio. One memo. One signature.
SIGNAL: learning is valued
new budget required: $0
Authorize the study-group documentation pathway.
Ten teachers, monthly, sixty minutes. One-page template: what was tried, what changed,
what students did differently, what surprised. Uses the existing PLC structure the district
already pays for. Counts toward licensure renewal hours.
SIGNAL: trying is valued
new budget required: $0
Add the AI-fluency increment tier at the next contract window.
One clause. Tied to demonstrated practice change — not seat-time, not certificate-collecting.
Peer-review mechanism borrowed from National Board Certification renewals,
reviewed by colleagues teaching the same subject.
SIGNAL: practice change is valued
new budget required: $0
— signed, the superintendent
Tue
Thu
The missing document is the one that says: we see you. This counts.
